text_original: Uhiveesal Lo?b (I) ' j The wise man who has charge of governing the empire j should know the cause of disorder before he can put it in order.
text_normalized: Uhiveesal Lo?b (I) ' j The wise man who has charge of governing the empire j should know the cause of disorder before he can put it in order.

text_original: Unless he knows its cause, he cannot regulate it.
text_normalized: Unless he knows its cause, he cannot regulate it.

text_original: It is similar to the problem of a physician who is attending a patient.
text_normalized: It is similar to the problem of a physician who is attending a patient.

text_original: He has to know the cause of the ailment before he can cure it.
text_normalized: He has to know the cause of the ailment before he can cure it.

text_original: Unless he knows its cause he cannot cure it.
text_normalized: Unless he knows its cause he cannot cure it.

text_original: How is the situation different for him who is to regulate disorder?
text_normalized: How is the situation different for him who is to regulate disorder?

text_original: He too has to know the cause of the disorder before he can regulate it.
text_normalized: He too has to know the cause of the disorder before he can regulate it.

text_original: Unless he knows its cause he cannot regulate it.
text_normalized: Unless he knows its cause he cannot regulate it.

text_original: The wise man who has charge of governing the empire must, then, investigate the cause of disorder.
text_normalized: The wise man who has charge of governing the empire must, then, investigate the cause of disorder.

text_original: Suppose we try to locate the cause of disorder, we shall j find it lies in the want of mutual love.
text_normalized: Suppose we try to locate the cause of disorder, we shall j find it lies in the want of mutual love.

text_original: What is called disorder; is just the lack of filial piety on the part of the minister: and the son towards the emperor and the father.
text_normalized: What is called disorder; is just the lack of filial piety on the part of the minister: and the son towards the emperor and the father.

text_original: As he loves himself and not his father the son benefits himself to the disadvantage of his father.
text_normalized: As he loves himself and not his father the son benefits himself to the disadvantage of his father.

text_original: As he lo'ves himself and not his elder brother, the younger brother benefits himself to the disadvantage of his elder brother.
text_normalized: As he lo'ves himself and not his elder brother, the younger brother benefits himself to the disadvantage of his elder brother.

text_original: As he loves himself and not his emperor, the minister benefits himself to the reading of that edition is also incorrect on a few points.
text_normalized: As he loves himself and not his emperor, the minister benefits himself to the reading of that edition is also incorrect on a few points.

text_original: / disadvantage of his emperor.
text_normalized: / disadvantage of his emperor.

text_original: And these are what is called disorder.
text_normalized: And these are what is called disorder.

text_original: When the father shows no affection to the son, when the elder brother shows no affection to the younger brother, and when the emperor shows no affection to the minister, on the other hand, it is also called disorder.
text_normalized: When the father shows no affection to the son, when the elder brother shows no affection to the younger brother, and when the emperor shows no affection to the minister, on the other hand, it is also called disorder.

text_original: When the father loves only himself and not the son, he benefits himself to the disadvantage of the son.
text_normalized: When the father loves only himself and not the son, he benefits himself to the disadvantage of the son.

text_original: When the elder brother loves only himself and not his younger brother, he benefits himself to the disadvantage of the younger brother.
text_normalized: When the elder brother loves only himself and not his younger brother, he benefits himself to the disadvantage of the younger brother.

text_original: When the emperor loves only himself and not his minister, he benefits himself to the disadvantage of his minister, and the reason for all these is want of mutual love.
text_normalized: When the emperor loves only himself and not his minister, he benefits himself to the disadvantage of his minister, and the reason for all these is want of mutual love.

text_original: This is true even among thieves and robbers.
text_normalized: This is true even among thieves and robbers.

text_original: As he loves only his own family and not other families, the thief steals from other families to profit his own family.
text_normalized: As he loves only his own family and not other families, the thief steals from other families to profit his own family.

text_original: As he loves only his own person and not others, the robber does violence to others to profit himself.
text_normalized: As he loves only his own person and not others, the robber does violence to others to profit himself.

text_original: And the reason for all this is want of love.
text_normalized: And the reason for all this is want of love.

text_original: This again is true in the mutual disturbance among the houses of the ministers and the mutual invasions among the states of the feudal lords.
text_normalized: This again is true in the mutual disturbance among the houses of the ministers and the mutual invasions among the states of the feudal lords.

text_original: As he loves only his own house and not the others, the minister disturbs the other houses to profit his own.
text_normalized: As he loves only his own house and not the others, the minister disturbs the other houses to profit his own.

text_original: As he loves only his own state and not the others, the feudal lord attacks the other states to profit his own.
text_normalized: As he loves only his own state and not the others, the feudal lord attacks the other states to profit his own.

text_original: These instances exhaust the confusion in the world.
text_normalized: These instances exhaust the confusion in the world.

text_original: And when we look into the causes we find they all arise from want of mutual love.
text_normalized: And when we look into the causes we find they all arise from want of mutual love.

text_original: Suppose everybody in the world loves universally, loving others as one’s self.
text_normalized: Suppose everybody in the world loves universally, loving others as one’s self.

text_original: Will there yet be any unfilial individual?
text_normalized: Will there yet be any unfilial individual?

text_original: When every one regards his father, elder brother, and emperor i as himself, whereto can he direct any unfilial feeling?
text_normalized: When every one regards his father, elder brother, and emperor i as himself, whereto can he direct any unfilial feeling?

text_original: Will there still be any unaffeetionate incliviclnal?
text_normalized: Will there still be any unaffeetionate incliviclnal?

text_original: When, every one regards his younger brother son., and niiiiistor as himself, whereto can he direct any disaffection?
text_normalized: When, every one regards his younger brother son., and niiiiistor as himself, whereto can he direct any disaffection?

text_original: Therefore there will not be any nnfilial feeling or disaffection.
text_normalized: Therefore there will not be any nnfilial feeling or disaffection.

text_original: Will there then be any thieves and robbers?
text_normalized: Will there then be any thieves and robbers?

text_original: When every one regards other families as his own family, who will steal?
text_normalized: When every one regards other families as his own family, who will steal?

text_original: When every one regards other persons as his own person, who will rob 1 Therefore there will not he any thieves or robbers.
text_normalized: When every one regards other persons as his own person, who will rob 1 Therefore there will not he any thieves or robbers.

text_original: Will there be mutual disturbance among the houses of the ministers and invasion among the states of the feudal lords?
text_normalized: Will there be mutual disturbance among the houses of the ministers and invasion among the states of the feudal lords?

text_original: When every one regards the houses of others as one’s own, who will he disturbing 1 When every one regards the states of others as one’s own, who will invade?
text_normalized: When every one regards the houses of others as one’s own, who will he disturbing 1 When every one regards the states of others as one’s own, who will invade?

text_original: Therefore there will be neither disturbances among the houses of the ministers nor invasion among the states of the feudal lords.
text_normalized: Therefore there will be neither disturbances among the houses of the ministers nor invasion among the states of the feudal lords.

text_original: If every one in the world will love uiiivervsally; states not attacking one another; houses not disturbing one another; thieves and robbers becoming extinct; emperor and ministers, fathers and sons, all being affectionate and filial — if all this comes to pass the world will be orderly.
text_normalized: If every one in the world will love uiiivervsally; states not attacking one another; houses not disturbing one another; thieves and robbers becoming extinct; emperor and ministers, fathers and sons, all being affectionate and filial — if all this comes to pass the world will be orderly.

text_original: Therefore, how can the wise man who has charge of governing the empire fail to restrain hate and encourage love?
text_normalized: Therefore, how can the wise man who has charge of governing the empire fail to restrain hate and encourage love?

text_original: So, when there is universal love in the world it will be orderly, and when there is mutual hate in the world it will be dis-orderly.
text_normalized: So, when there is universal love in the world it will be orderly, and when there is mutual hate in the world it will be dis-orderly.

text_original: This is why Motse insisted on persuading people to love others.
text_normalized: This is why Motse insisted on persuading people to love others.
